title: 9 Stocks Offering Up to 46% Upside Despite a Hawkish Fed
author: David Wagner
description: Following the FOMC meeting led by Kevin Warsh, the Federal Reserve signaled a hawkish stance with nine policymakers now expecting rate hikes by end of 2026, contrary to earlier expectations. The Fed raised its inflation outlook to 3.6% and left rates unchanged at 3.50%-3.75%. The article identifies nine U.S. stocks with strong fundamentals, free cash flow yields above 5%, and EPS growth exceeding 10% that are well-positioned to navigate a higher-rate environment, offering upside potential of 27-46%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest U.S. natural gas producer with strong Q1 2026 free cash flow of $1.8 billion and 57% YoY revenue growth. Benefits from commodity-driven earnings less dependent on economic cycles and growing demand from LNG exports and data centers in a higher-rate environment.

Operates Futubull and Moomoo trading platforms with business model well-suited for higher interest rates. Significant revenue from interest income supports profitability when rates are elevated, with strong earnings growth and record trading activity in late 2025.

title: My ’Do Nothing’ Stocks Just Might Do Something
author: John Dorfman
description: Value investor John Dorfman highlights five underperforming stocks that have remained flat over the past year but show promise for future growth. These 'Do-Nothing Club' stocks include Raymond James Financial, Gentex Corp, EQT Corp, Banner Corp, and Korn Ferry, which Dorfman believes are overlooked by analysts despite strong fundamentals. Historically, his Do-Nothing Club recommendations have averaged 16.6% annual returns versus 10.4% for the S&P 500.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has generated no return in the past year despite 300% growth over the past decade. Company has maintained profitability for 30 consecutive years with impressive recent profits. Author believes analysts are undervaluing the stock due to overemphasis on recent performance.

Earnings rose over 13% in the past four quarters while stock price remained flat. Company owns over 1,700 patents and is nearly debt-free, which is rare in today's market. Author finds it surprising the stock hasn't moved given strong fundamentals.

Largest natural-gas producer in Appalachian Basin trading at attractive 11x earnings valuation. Author expects natural gas to gain market share from oil due to Middle East geopolitical tensions affecting oil prices and refinery capacity.

Trades below 11x earnings with minimal debt (only 6% of equity). Despite investor concerns about commercial real estate exposure and slow growth, company has been profitable 23 of past 25 years, with only Great Recession exceptions.
Business consulting and executive-search firm with 10%+ annual profit growth over the past decade. Trades at attractive multiples (1.2x revenue, 13x earnings) and maintains a 16-year profit streak.

title: This $4 Million NMI Holdings Sale Comes as the Insurer Grows Book Value 15%
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Muhlenkamp & Co. sold 101,570 shares of NMI Holdings (worth $3.93 million) in Q1 2026, reducing its position from 5.1% to 3.62% of AUM. Despite NMI's strong fundamentals—including 15% year-over-year book value growth, $99.3 million Q1 net income, and improved efficiency metrics—the stock has remained flat over the past year, significantly underperforming the S&P 500. The sale may reflect opportunity cost allocation rather than a negative view on the company's performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While NMI demonstrates strong operational fundamentals (15% book value growth, 6% revenue increase, improved expense ratios, and solid profitability), the stock's flat performance over the past year despite these gains suggests market skepticism. The fund's sale appears driven by opportunity costs rather than company weakness, indicating neutral sentiment—the company is performing well but the market hasn't rewarded it.
Listed as Muhlenkamp's second-largest holding at $25.54 million (6.7% of AUM), indicating the fund manager's confidence in the company's prospects.
Muhlenkamp's top holding at $28.13 million (7.4% of AUM), suggesting strong conviction in the company's value.
Third-largest holding at $25.03 million (6.6% of AUM), indicating the fund manager's positive outlook on the company.

title: Arium Networks Launches Following Completion of EQT's Acquisition of Crown Castle's Small Cell Solutions Business
author: Not Specified
description: Arium Networks officially launched as a standalone company following EQT's acquisition of Crown Castle's Small Cell & Venue business. The new digital infrastructure platform operates over 100,000 small cells across 43 states, serving the three largest U.S. mobile network operators and focusing on enhancing wireless connectivity in dense, high-demand areas.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Successfully completed acquisition of Crown Castle's Small Cell business and launched Arium Networks as a standalone company, demonstrating successful deal execution and investment in growing digital infrastructure sector.
Divested its Small Cell & Venue business to EQT; this represents a strategic portfolio decision rather than negative news, as the company is focusing on other infrastructure assets.

title: The Oil World Is Splintering — And America Has The Backup Plan
author: Stjepan Kalinic
description: The Iran war has disrupted global oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, forcing Asia to seek alternative sources. The U.S. has emerged as a critical backup supplier, with record crude production of 13.6 million barrels per day and total liquid fuels production of 24 million barrels per day. American energy companies are benefiting from increased exports, while new supply chains involving Venezuelan heavy crude and Russian fuel oil are reshaping global energy dependencies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Hit an all-time high of $176.41 in late March; benefiting from record U.S. crude production and increased global demand for American oil exports as alternative to Middle Eastern supplies.
Reported superb Q1 results with 413% year-over-year growth in earnings-per-share; positioned to benefit from increased natural gas and energy production demand amid global supply disruptions.
